models.generation_context: 2nd-gen (L663), introduced 2020, 2026 refresh adds 13.1" Pivi Pro and restyled lights
models.model_summary: Two-door 5-seat short-wheelbase Defender, offered for 2026 US market exclusively in V8 form (the previous P300 and P400 90 trims were dropped for MY26). Powered by the 5.0L supercharged V8 (518 hp) with eight-speed automatic, twin-speed transfer case, and standard AWD.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Consumer Reports expects below-average reliability based on Land Rover brand history; KBB owner reviews trend more positive at 4.2 / 5.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Defender 90 or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers love the Defender 90's distinctive 2-door styling, supercharged V8 thunder, and uncompromised off-road hardware. Critiques cite the high MSRP versus the 110, modest interior cargo space, and the now-V8-only lineup that pushes the entry price well above six figures.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the V8 90's iconic styling, off-road capability, and unique character. Common complaints involve high price relative to interior practicality and reliability concerns historically common to JLR products.

images.credit: Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC

trims.notes: Sole-trim model — for 2026 US market the Defender 90 is offered exclusively in V8 form (P300 and P400 90 trims dropped). Sole-trim atomic rule applied: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null.

models.notes: Single 2026 trim (V8 only). The Defender 90 P300 and P400 versions previously offered have been dropped for 2026 US market. 2026 refresh adds 13.1-inch Pivi Pro infotainment, restyled LED headlights with signature graphics, flush smoked taillights, and new paint options. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the 2026 Defender 90.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen (L663), introduced 2020, 2026 refresh adds 13.1" Pivi Pro and restyled lights
models.model_summary: Four-door 5- or 5+2-seat midsize Defender. 2026 US lineup offers three powertrain lines: P300 2.0L turbo I4 (296 hp), P400 3.0L MHEV I6 (395 hp), and P525 5.0L supercharged V8 (518 hp). Five trims (S, X-Dynamic SE, X, Trophy Edition, V8). 2026 brings the 13.1-inch Pivi Pro touchscreen and exterior light refresh.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. CR expects below-average reliability based on brand history; KBB owner reviews are more positive at ~4.2/5.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Defender 110 or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: The 2026 Defender 110 is the most-praised configuration in the lineup — reviewers highlight quiet on-road comfort, genuine off-road capability, and a striking interior. The 2026 refresh's 13.1-inch screen and updated lights are welcomed. Critiques cite climbing MSRPs across trims and pre-Pivi Pro user-interface holdovers on some controls.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise styling, comfort, off-road capability, and interior; complaints commonly focus on reliability concerns, fuel economy, and high cost of ownership.

trims.notes: Base of P525 V8 powertrain line. Singleton trim_family (only V8 trim of the Defender 110). 5+2 third-row not offered on V8.

models.notes: Five-trim 2026 lineup across three powertrain lines: P300 (S only), P400 MHEV (X-Dynamic SE, Trophy Edition, X), and P525 V8 (V8). Per multi-powertrain rule, S, X-Dynamic SE, and V8 are each is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null. Trophy Edition offered in two color choices at the same MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the 2026 Defender. 2026 refresh adds 13.1-inch Pivi Pro touchscreen, restyled LED headlights, and new paint options.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen (L663), 130 long-wheelbase variant introduced 2023, 2026 refresh adds 13.1" Pivi Pro and restyled lights
models.model_summary: Long-wheelbase 8-passenger three-row Defender, sharing its 119-in wheelbase with the 110 but extending overall length to ~211 in for a full-size third row. 2026 US lineup spans five trims across three powertrain lines: P300 2.0L turbo I4 (296 hp) standard on S, P400 3.0L MHEV I6 (395 hp) on X-Dynamic SE/Outbound/X, and P525 5.0L supercharged V8 (518 hp) on the top V8 trim. Captain chairs now optional in 2nd row.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. CR projects below-average reliability for the Defender family based on brand history.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Defender 130 or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers value the Defender 130 as the most family-friendly Defender, offering a true adult-sized third row that the 110's 5+2 can't match, with the same Defender off-road hardware and 2026 refresh updates. Critiques target the 130 V8's price approaching Range Rover money, ride quirks on rough surfaces, and the still-low EPA combined ratings.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner praise centers on the genuinely usable third row and the V8's character; complaints involve reliability fears, fuel economy, and pricing creep on top trims.

notable_other: 8-passenger standard seating (2nd-row captain chairs now optional for 2026)

images.credit: Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC

Per landroverusa.com models-and-specs the 130 S is labeled '3.0L 6-Cylinder' so engine_config set to I6 (296 hp output). 19-inch wheels standard.

trims.notes: Base of P500 V8 powertrain line within Defender 130. Singleton trim_family. The Defender 130 V8 uses Land Rover's P500-spec tuning of the 5.0L supercharged V8 (493 hp / 450 lb-ft) versus the 90/110 P525 (518 hp / 461 lb-ft) per landroverusa.com models-and-specs.

models.notes: Five-trim 2026 lineup across three powertrain lines: P300 (S), P400 MHEV (X-Dynamic SE, Outbound, X), and P500 V8 (V8). Per multi-powertrain rule, S, X-Dynamic SE, and V8 are each is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null. The 130 V8 uses the lower P500 tune of the 5.0L V8 (493 hp) versus the 90/110's P525 (518 hp). Outbound is the only 5-passenger 130 (no 3rd row). Captain chairs new option for 2026 2nd row. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the 2026 Defender 130.

models.generation_context: 2nd-gen Defender (L663) high-performance Octa variant introduced 2024, sold on its own landroverusa.com showroom page
models.model_summary: High-performance flagship variant of the Defender 110, powered exclusively by BMW-sourced 4.4L twin-turbocharged V8 (P635) producing 626 hp and 553 lb-ft of torque. Adds 6D Dynamics hydraulically interconnected air suspension, wider track, unique fender flares, and Octa-specific off-road tuning. Two trims: Octa and Octa Black.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Low US volume and brand history yield low/unknown confidence.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Defender Octa or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Defender Octa as a remarkable combination of supercar pace and genuine off-road durability — its 6D Dynamics hydraulic suspension and 626-hp BMW V8 deliver a unique character. Critiques cite the steep entry price, fuel economy, and that the Octa's hardware advantages overlap a niche the standard 110 V8 already partially fills.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review samples are very small (limited US volume). Available owner feedback praises the bespoke nature, V8 character, and off-road capability versus stock 110.

notable_other: Body-On-Frame Octa Mode for high-speed off-road
Octa-exclusive wider track and fender flares
22-inch Style 7026 Gloss Black wheels standard (20-inch available)
Body Health Monitoring with G-force telemetry

images.credit: Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC

trims.notes: Base trim of Defender Octa model (split as separate model per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule — landroverusa.com markets Octa on its own showroom page at /defender/defender-octa/). BMW-sourced 4.4L twin-turbo V8 shared with related JLR/BMW applications. 6D Dynamics interconnected hydraulic suspension is Octa-exclusive.

notable_other: Narvik Black exterior + Gloss Black detailing
20-inch wheels with All-Terrain tires (vs 22-inch on base Octa)

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family. Powertrain identical to Octa (BMW 4.4L twin-turbo V8, 626 hp). Switches to 20-in All-Terrain tires by default versus the Octa's 22-in pavement-oriented setup.

models.notes: Two-trim model (Octa, Octa Black) split as separate model per perf-variant rule — landroverusa.com markets Defender Octa on its own showroom page at /defender/defender-octa/. BMW-sourced 4.4L twin-turbo V8 P635 (626 hp / 553 lb-ft). 6D Dynamics interconnected hydraulic air suspension is Octa-exclusive. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested. Both trims share EPA ID 49591 (Defender 110 MHEV 4.4L V8) per EPA filings.

models.model_summary: Three-row 7-passenger midsize luxury SUV with traditional Land Rover off-road capability. 2026 lineup spans four trims across two powertrain lines: P300 2.0L turbo I4 (296 hp) for S and Dynamic SE; P360 3.0L 48V mild-hybrid I6 (355 hp) standard on Gemini and Tempest editions.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Land Rover brand historically rates near the bottom of mainstream reliability surveys.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Discovery or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers note the 2026 Discovery delivers a genuinely capable, comfortable seven-seat off-road package with strong towing and traditional Land Rover engineering. Common critiques are dated infotainment (still pre-Pivi Pro on some screens before the 2024 refresh updates), divisive rear-end styling, and significant equipment gaps versus newer rivals at this price.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise off-road capability, towing, and seven-seat practicality; complaints commonly cite reliability worries, electronics glitches, and fuel economy.

notable_other: Wading depth 35.4 inches with optional air suspension

Per multi-powertrain rule, S and Gemini Edition are both is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null. New 13.1-inch Pivi Pro screen for 2026 (refresh). NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the 2026 Discovery.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen, introduced 2015 (final years of original L550 platform before successor)
models.model_summary: Compact 5+2 luxury crossover offered for 2026 as a single Landmark Edition trim. Powered by a 2.0L turbo I4 with 246 hp and a 9-speed automatic with standard AWD. Carryover platform with an aging cabin, but retains Land Rover off-road hardware and Terrain Response.

reliability.summary: No model-specific JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published for the 2026 MY. Land Rover brand has historically ranked near the bottom of mass-market reliability surveys; owner reviews for this generation flag engine lag concerns.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Discovery Sport or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the 2026 Discovery Sport as visually appealing with credible Land Rover off-road DNA, but criticize the aging platform, modest fuel economy, and entry-level interior versus newer rivals. The simplified single-Landmark-trim lineup adds standard equipment but does not address the underlying old architecture.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise comfort, spaciousness, and ADAS suite; common complaints center on fuel economy, engine lag, and reliability/repair experience.

notable_other: Cabin Air Purification Plus with CO2 monitoring
3D Surround Camera with ClearSight Ground View

images.credit: Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC
Jaguar Land Rover North America, LLC

trims.notes: Sole 2026 trim — Land Rover consolidated the Discovery Sport lineup to a single Landmark Edition for 2026 (dropped previous S / Dynamic SE / Dynamic HSE). Sole-trim atomic rule applied: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null. Image URLs are landroverusa.com consumer-page URLs flagged needs_scraping for follow-up CDN extraction (page does not expose direct asset URLs to WebFetch). NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested any current Discovery Sport. Optional 5+2 third-row package available; 0-60 of 7.5 sec applies to 5+2 configuration. Base Landmark wheels are 19in Style 5089.

models.notes: Single-trim lineup for 2026 (Landmark Edition only). NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the 2026 Discovery Sport. Land Rover brand-wide warranty: 4yr/50k bumper-to-bumper + powertrain, 6yr/unlimited corrosion, 4yr/50k roadside, 5yr/60k complimentary scheduled maintenance.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen (L460), introduced 2022. 2026 adds new flagship SV Black trim and refreshed P550e PHEV across the lineup
models.model_summary: Flagship full-size luxury SUV available in Standard (SWB) and Long Wheelbase (LWB) configurations, with 4-, 5-, or 7-seat layouts. 2026 lineup spans four trims (SE, Autobiography, SV, SV Black) across three powertrain lines: P400 3.0L 48V MHEV I6 (395 hp), P530 4.4L V8 twin-turbo MHEV (523 hp), P550e PHEV (542 hp combined, 50-mi EV range), and P615 SV/SV Black V8 (606 hp). New for 2026: SV Black trim joins the lineup as Range Rover's darkest, most stealth-spec V8 variant.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Land Rover brand has historically rated near the bottom of mainstream reliability rankings.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Range Rover or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rank the 2026 Range Rover among the world's best luxury SUVs — praising the imperious driving position, near-silent ride, cutting-edge tech, and exceptional rear-seat experience on LWB models. Critiques focus on the steep MSRP curve toward SV, predicted Land Rover reliability, and a Pivi Pro interface still missing certain physical controls.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner sentiment trends positive on luxury, presence, and capability; persistent complaints involve reliability, dealer service, and software/electronics glitches.

notable_other: All-Wheel Steering with rear-axle steering up to 7.3 degrees
Available as SWB (5-seat) or LWB (7-seat) — LWB 7-seat starts at $120,700

trims.notes: Base of P550e PHEV powertrain line. Sole-trim-family for PHEV powertrain within Range Rover (no SE PHEV — Autobiography is the entry PHEV). 38.2 kWh total battery / 31.8 kWh usable per Land Rover technical data; battery_capacity_kwh records total per spec §3.5. EPA has not yet published a discrete fuelyeconomy.gov page for the 2026 P550e — source set to brand-make-page per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback. Land Rover-cited 22 mpg combined (charge-sustaining) and 53 MPGe (charge-depleting). Available as SWB ($159,200) or LWB ($177,900 7-seat / $179,500 standard 4-seat).

The Range Rover SV variant is marketed as a trim with its own Range Rover SV destination page (rangerover.com/en-us/range-rover/sv.html) but remains within Range Rover model per landroverusa.com models-and-specifications page which lists SV alongside SE/Autobiography. P615 = 606 hp retuned version of the M177 4.4L V8 shared with Range Rover Sport SV. Sole-trim base for SV line (SV Black is a step-up variant).

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family. New flagship variant for 2026 — replaces SVAutobiography Ultimate as the ultra-luxury stealth-spec. Powertrain identical to SV (P615 606 hp). Available SWB or LWB.

models.notes: Four-trim 2026 lineup (SE, Autobiography, SV, SV Black) across three powertrain lines: P400 MHEV (SE), P550e PHEV (Autobiography), P530 V8 MHEV (available as option on SE/Autobiography ICE), and P615 SV V8 (SV, SV Black). Per multi-powertrain-rule, SE / Autobiography / SV are all is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null. SV Black is the only step-up. Each trim is offered in SWB and LWB configurations with LWB pricing $7,400-$22,000 higher than SWB. LWB Autobiography 7-seat config $177,900. New for 2026: SV Black variant joins the lineup. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested. EPA fueleconomy.gov IDs 50014 / 50015 / 49916 / 49917 / 49920 / 49915 all verified for the 2026 model year.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen, introduced 2022. 2026 lineup retains the Battersea Edition special and adds a 20th-anniversary Twenty Edition
models.model_summary: Sport-flavored full-size luxury SUV. 5-passenger configuration. 2026 lineup spans five trims (SE, Dynamic SE, Battersea Edition, Twenty Edition, Autobiography) across three powertrain lines: P360 3.0L 48V MHEV I6 (355 hp), P400 3.0L 48V MHEV I6 (395 hp), P530 4.4L V8 twin-turbo (523 hp), and P550e PHEV (542 hp combined, ~51 mi EV range). High-perf P635 SV variants split as separate model.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Land Rover brand has historically rated near the bottom of mainstream reliability surveys.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Range Rover Sport or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers value the 2026 Range Rover Sport as a more athletic alternative to the standard Range Rover, with sharper handling, available 6D Dynamics pitch control, and high-end interior fittings — while retaining genuine off-road hardware. Critiques target ride harshness on 23-inch wheels and Land Rover's predicted reliability.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner sentiment tracks the standard Range Rover — strong praise for luxury and driving feel, with reliability and software complaints recurring.

Powertrain defaults to P530 V8 per landroverusa.com models-and-specs; PHEV variant Autobiography increases cost. delta_from_base references Twenty Edition (same P530 powertrain) for cleanest comparison.

models.notes: Five-trim 2026 lineup (SE, Dynamic SE, Battersea Edition, Twenty Edition, Autobiography) across three primary powertrain lines: P360 MHEV (SE), P400 MHEV (Dynamic SE), P530 V8 (Twenty Edition, Autobiography option), P550e PHEV (Battersea Edition, Autobiography option). Per multi-powertrain rule SE, Dynamic SE, Battersea Edition are each is_base_trim true. Twenty Edition and Autobiography are step-ups within the P530 V8 line. SV trims (SV / SV Black / SV Carbon) split as separate Range Rover Sport SV model per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested. EPA IDs 50165 / 50166 / 49918 / 49919 verified for 2026 model year.

models.generation_context: High-performance variant of 3rd-gen Range Rover Sport (L461). 2026 lineup retains SV, SV Black, and adds SV Carbon
models.model_summary: Ultimate performance variant of the Range Rover Sport, split as a separate model per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule (rangerover.com markets SV on its own destination page). Three trims (SV, SV Black, SV Carbon) all powered by the P635 BMW-derived 4.4L twin-turbo V8 (626 hp / 553 lb-ft), with 3.6-second 0-60, 180-mph top speed, and 6D Dynamics hydraulically interconnected suspension.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Low US volume and brand history yield low/unknown confidence.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Range Rover Sport SV or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Range Rover Sport SV as the world's most accomplished performance SUV — combining 626 hp BMW V8 thrust, sophisticated 6D Dynamics suspension, and SUV practicality. Comparisons with Cayenne Turbo GT, BMW XM, and Lamborghini Urus position SV at the luxury-performance pinnacle. Critiques cite the steep price and questionable necessity over standard Range Rover Sport.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited owner-review sample size due to low US volume and recent introduction.

notable_other: P635 BMW-derived 4.4L twin-turbo V8 (626 hp / 553 lb-ft)
SV Mode with red cabin illumination
Performance ceramic brake option (carbon-ceramic on SV Carbon)

trims.notes: Base trim of the Range Rover Sport SV model. Split as separate model per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule — rangerover.com markets Range Rover Sport SV on its own destination page (rangerover.com/en-us/range-rover-sport/sv.html). BMW-sourced 4.4L twin-turbo V8 shared with X5/X7 M60i and Range Rover SV. EPA labels this trim as 'Range Rover Sport SVR MHEV' (ID 49919) — the EPA filing terminology is the BMW Sport Variant Race designation that JLR markets as SV in retail.

Same P635 V8 performance hardware as SV

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family. Top-spec lightweight SV variant. Powertrain identical to SV (P635 V8).

models.notes: Three-trim model split from Range Rover Sport per spec §6.4 perf-variant rule — rangerover.com markets the Sport SV on its own destination page (rangerover.com/en-us/range-rover-sport/sv.html). All three share the P635 BMW-derived 4.4L twin-turbo V8 (626 hp / 553 lb-ft, 0-60 in 3.6 s, 180-mph top speed). 6D Dynamics hydraulically interconnected suspension is SV-exclusive. EPA fileconomy.gov ID 49919 (labeled 'Range Rover Sport SVR MHEV') verified for the 2026 model year — Land Rover retail branding is 'SV', EPA filing uses the BMW-shared SVR designation. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested.

models.model_summary: Midsize 5-passenger luxury crossover with coupe-influenced proportions. 2026 lineup offers four trims (S, Belgravia Edition, Autobiography, Belgravia Edition Satin) across two powertrain lines: P250 2.0L turbo I4 (247 hp) and P400 3.0L 48V mild-hybrid I6 (395 hp). Available P340 with the I6 detuned to 335 hp on certain trims.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Consumer Reports lists overall reliability as low based on prior-year Velar data and Land Rover brand history.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Range Rover Velar or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers cite the 2026 Velar's sleek design, refined interior, and quiet ride as strengths, with the P400 powertrain praised for effortless thrust. Common critiques are the cramped rear seat, modest cargo capacity for the price, and below-average expected reliability.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited 2026 owner-review sample size at research time. Common owner praise centers on exterior styling and Pivi Pro infotainment; complaints typically involve electrical glitches and ride harshness on larger wheels.

notable_other: Cabin Air Purification Plus with CO2 monitoring

trims.notes: Singleton trim_family. Powertrain identical to Autobiography (P400 MHEV I6). Limited 1-of-400 production run for 2026.

models.notes: Four-trim 2026 lineup across two powertrain lines: P250 (S, Belgravia Edition) and P400 MHEV (Autobiography, Belgravia Edition Satin). Belgravia Edition can optionally be configured with the P400 engine. Per spec multi-powertrain-line rule, S and Autobiography are both is_base_trim true with delta_from_base null. P340 powertrain (EPA ID 48991, 335 hp) listed by EPA but per landroverusa.com marketing page, only P250 and P400 are configurable for 2026 US trims — P340 retained on EPA listing may be carryover or fleet/order-only and is not split as a separate trim. NHTSA/IIHS have not tested the 2026 Velar.

models.model_summary: Compact 5-passenger luxury crossover positioned as the entry to the Range Rover family. 2026 lineup simplified to two trims (S and Hoxton Edition), both powered by the P250 2.0L turbo I4 with 246 hp and 9-speed automatic AWD.

reliability.summary: No 2026 JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted reliability score published. Edmunds and Consumer Reports flag below-average reliability expectations for the Evoque based on Land Rover brand history; electrical and stop-start system issues common in owner reports.

customer_satisfaction.summary: The JD Power 2026 US APEAL Study has not yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (scheduled release: July 21, 2026). No 2026 APEAL data exists yet for the Land Rover Range Rover Evoque or for the Land Rover brand overall.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers credit the 2026 Evoque with stylish exterior design, refined interior materials, and credible off-road ability for its segment. Critiques cite uneven driving manners (turbo lag plus jerky stop-start), modest power for the price, and aging mechanicals versus newer compact luxury rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the styling, interior comfort, and off-road capability. Frequent complaints involve electrical glitches, turbo lag at launch, stop-start hesitation, and brake feel.

notable_other: Cabin Air Purification Plus with CO2 monitoring

models.notes: Two-trim 2026 lineup (S, Hoxton Edition). Both share P250 2.0L turbo I4, 246 hp, 9-speed auto, AWD. Hoxton Edition replaces the previous Dynamic SE trim.
